deck_prompt_meta.design_system_name: Navy Editorial Report — Cool Lavender + Deep Navy
deck_prompt_meta.global_theme_summary: A restrained, research-report visual system. Cool pale-lavender canvas (~#E9EDF6 approx) with a single deep-navy accent (~#1C2C5A approx) and a medium blue-grey for secondary text (~#7C8AAE approx). All slides use a Pretendard-family sans-serif with strong weight contrast (ExtraBold for titles, Light/Regular for body). The deck reads like an internal proposal document for a university research course — formal, evidence-led, and quietly confident. There are no warm colors, no gradients, no shadows, and no decorative shapes that do not appear in the original reference deck.
global_consistency_rules: Color palette is fixed at four colors: deep navy (#1C2C5A approx) for primary text and emphasis, pale lavender (#C9D1E5 approx) for secondary panels and decorative bands, medium blue-grey (#7C8AAE approx) for secondary text, white (#FFFFFF) for cards and reverse-text. The canvas itself is a very pale lavender-blue (#E9EDF6 approx). No new accent colors are introduced anywhere in the deck.

Typography is locked to a Pretendard-style Korean/Latin sans-serif. Title-case headings use ExtraBold/Bold weights at large size; body copy uses Light/Regular at smaller size; ALL CAPS kickers use Bold or SemiBold with slightly loosened tracking. Plain black (#000) is never used for body text — always deep navy.
Number badges (01, 02, 03 …) always render as a deep navy filled square with bold white numerals. The badge size, corner radius, and font weight are identical across the entire deck.
Page numbers always sit in the bottom-right corner accompanied by a small navy accent square (~4–6px). The bottom-left corner of every body page is intentionally left empty.
The diagonal lavender-triangular overlay motif appears only on the cover (slide 1) and the closing (slide 13). Body pages keep a clean canvas — never reuse the diagonal motif on body slides.
Diagram connector lines and arrows share the same stroke weight (~1.0–1.5px equivalent) and the same deep-navy color across every page that contains a diagram.
Icons live only in the top-right corner of cards. All icons share a single line weight, a single color (deep navy or medium blue-grey), and a single approximate size. Filled-style icons are not used.
Cards use a white fill with a thin lavender-grey outline (#D6DCE9 approx). Card outline weight, corner radius, and internal padding are identical across every body slide that uses cards.
Tables follow a single style: deep navy filled header row with white text and centered alignment; body rows on white with very thin lavender-grey grid lines and left-aligned text labels.
Photographs and illustrations are not used. The deck is entirely typography, geometry, and minimal iconography.
deck_prompt_meta.body_slide_system: Every body slide (slides 2–12) follows a fixed three-zone composition. (Header zone, top ~12–15%): a thin full-width deep-navy bar runs across the very top of the page; below it, the slide's Title Case heading sits left-aligned in large ExtraBold deep navy, paired with an ALL CAPS section kicker right-aligned in small bold deep navy; directly beneath the kicker, a two-line right-aligned subtitle in medium blue-grey gives a one-sentence frame for the slide. (Body zone, middle ~70–75%): the only zone that varies across slides — it is filled by exactly one of the nine allowed layout families (cover / contents-left-panel / insight-claim-deep-navy-block / insight-claim-white-card / comparison-two-column-cards / process-hub-and-spoke / data-gantt-table / metric-numbered-card-grid / closing-end-page). (Footer zone, bottom ~5–8%): a small page-number numeral sits in the bottom-right corner, flanked by a small navy accent square; the bottom-left corner is empty. The header and footer never change across slides 2–12 in any visible dimension. Slide 1 (cover) and slide 13 (closing) explicitly break out of this body system and follow their own title-page / closing-page logic.

slides.slide_title: YouthPath — A Multi-Agent Assistant for Youth Social Entry

prompt.objective: Open the recorded video presentation with a single frame that establishes the project name, the team's affiliation, the submission date, and the four core technologies — without introducing any element that will not reappear elsewhere in the deck.
prompt.narrative_function: Set tone and authority. The cover is the only slide besides the closing that uses the diagonal lavender overlay, so it must visually anchor the deck's opening before any body content arrives.
prompt.visual_intent: Restrained editorial cover. The viewer should feel they are looking at a research report's title page rather than a marketing landing page. The diagonal overlay is a quiet background motif, never the focal point.

content_blocks.purpose: State the project's English subtitle and name in two stacked lines that read as a single phrase.
content_blocks.content_instruction: Line 1 (lighter weight): 'A Multi-Agent Assistant for'. Line 2 (heaviest weight, larger): 'Youth Social Entry'. Both lines left-aligned, deep navy.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Center-left of the slide. The title block occupies roughly the left 1/2 to 2/3 of the slide width, vertically centered.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Pretendard ExtraBold for line 2, Pretendard Light/Regular for line 1. Tight leading between the two lines. Deep navy color. Title case (no all-caps).

content_blocks.purpose: Identify the project shortname in formal/categorical voice.
content_blocks.content_instruction: Single line: 'YOUTHPATH — INITIAL PROPOSAL'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Directly below the title block, left-aligned to the same left margin.
content_blocks.style_instruction: ALL CAPS, small size (~roughly 1/4 to 1/5 of the title height), Bold or SemiBold, medium blue-grey color, slightly loosened letter-spacing.

content_blocks.purpose: Identify the institution, the team, and the four core technologies.
content_blocks.content_instruction: Three short stacked lines: 'Hanyang University · Deep Learning Methods and Application' / 'Five-Person Team' / 'LLM · RAG · Multi-Agent · MCP'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Bottom-right corner of the slide, right-aligned.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Small Pretendard Regular, deep navy. Lines are tight together. A small deep-navy filled accent square (~4–6px) sits just below the lowest line, also right-aligned.

content_blocks.content_instruction: Single line: '2026.05.05'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Top-right corner of the slide.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Small Bold deep navy. Right-aligned.

content_blocks.purpose: Vertical chapter-style label running down the left edge — the cover's signature element from the reference 01.png.
content_blocks.content_instruction: Single rotated word: 'PRESENTATION'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Far left edge of the slide, rotated 90 degrees counter-clockwise so the word reads bottom-to-top. Vertically centered along the left edge.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Small ALL CAPS, Bold, deep navy, slightly loosened letter-spacing.

content_blocks.purpose: Set the cover's atmospheric background using the reference deck's diagonal overlay motif.
content_blocks.content_instruction: Two or three asymmetric large triangles in pale lavender (~#C9D1E5 approx) and a slightly lighter shade. They form a diagonal cut across the right third of the slide, never overlapping the title text.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Right third of the slide as a background layer. Triangles extend off the right and bottom edges. Always sit behind all text.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Flat fills only. No gradients, no shadows, no outlines. Low contrast against the pale lavender canvas.

layout_instructions.structure: Cover layout: a single composition with the title block as the visual anchor on the center-left and the diagonal overlay on the right. Vertical 'PRESENTATION' label on the far left edge. Date in the top-right. Metadata in the bottom-right.

header_body_footer.header: Thin full-width deep-navy bar across the very top edge. Date '2026.05.05' in the top-right.
header_body_footer.body: Title block (two lines) center-left. Subtitle 'YOUTHPATH — INITIAL PROPOSAL' directly below. Vertical 'PRESENTATION' label rotated on the left edge. Diagonal lavender triangles in the right third as background.
header_body_footer.footer: Three-line metadata block in the bottom-right with a small deep-navy accent square. The cover does not display a page number.

layout_instructions.alignment: Title and subtitle: left-aligned to a shared left margin (roughly 7% from the left edge). Date and metadata: right-aligned to a shared right margin. Vertical label: hugs the very left edge.
layout_instructions.spacing: Generous whitespace. Approximately the left half of the slide is dedicated to the title block and its surrounding air. The right half holds the diagonal overlay and the date.
layout_instructions.body_slide_consistency: The cover deliberately does not follow the body-slide header rule. It is the only slide other than the closing where the diagonal overlay appears. No page number on this slide.

palette_rules: Use only the four deck colors: pale-lavender canvas, deep navy, medium blue-grey, pale lavender (overlay).
Diagonal overlay must be in pale lavender — never a saturated or warm color.
Background canvas is the deck's standard pale lavender-blue — not pure white.
typography_rules: Title line 2 is the largest type element on the entire deck.
Title line 1 is roughly 50–60% the optical size of line 2 and a markedly lighter weight.
ALL CAPS subtitle uses loosened letter-spacing.
No italic, no underline anywhere on the cover.
component_rules: Vertical 'PRESENTATION' label is rotated exactly 90 degrees, not 270.
The small deep-navy accent square in the bottom-right is the same accent square that will reappear next to every body-slide page number — establish its size here.
icon_rules: No icons on the cover beyond the rotated word label.
infographic_rules: No infographic content on the cover.
diagram_rules: No diagrams on the cover.
imagery_rules: No illustrations beyond the diagonal triangle overlay.
Triangles must be flat fills, no gradients.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Centering the title block dead-center of the slide (kills the editorial asymmetry).
Using the title in ALL CAPS (would conflict with the body-slide title style).
Adding a logo, icon, or illustration of a person.
Overlapping the diagonal triangles with the title text.
Adding a page number to the cover.
Adding bullet points or a contents preview to the cover.

must_include: Hanyang University and the course name
evidence_to_avoid: Any source content — the cover should not summarize the proposal.

generator_notes.ppt_generation_note: When generated as a PPT slide, the diagonal overlay can be rendered as two or three flat-fill triangle shapes layered behind a transparent background; do not use gradient fills.
generator_notes.image_generation_note: When generated as a flat image, treat the diagonal triangles as a single composed background motif. Avoid lens flares, glow effects, or any 3D treatment.
generator_notes.fallback_if_content_is_sparse: Content is fixed and minimal by design. If the generator wants to add filler, suppress it — the cover's restraint is intentional.

prompt.objective: Give the audience a six-section map of the deck so they can mentally navigate the talk and so the evaluator can locate which slides answer which rubric criteria.
prompt.narrative_function: Open the body of the deck. Set expectations about what is coming and how it is structured. This is the only body slide that uses the left-panel variant.
prompt.visual_intent: Two-zone composition. The left third feels like an editorial chapter divider in pale lavender; the right two-thirds feels like a tidy numbered list of chapters. The whole slide should read as 'opening table of contents,' not as a content slide carrying argument.

content_blocks.purpose: Anchor the page with the chapter-header word 'Contents'.
content_blocks.content_instruction: Single word: 'Contents'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Inside the left 1/3 lavender panel, vertically centered.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Very large Pretendard ExtraBold, deep navy, title case.

content_blocks.purpose: Identify the deck context in formal voice.
content_blocks.content_instruction: Single line: 'YOUTHPATH PROPOSAL'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Directly below the 'Contents' title inside the left panel.
content_blocks.style_instruction: ALL CAPS, small Pretendard Bold or SemiBold, medium blue-grey, slightly loosened tracking.

content_blocks.purpose: Provide the standard right-aligned ALL CAPS section kicker that appears on every body slide.
content_blocks.content_instruction: Single line: 'CONTENTS'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Top-right corner, just below the deep-navy header bar.
content_blocks.style_instruction: ALL CAPS, small Pretendard Bold, deep navy, right-aligned.

content_blocks.purpose: List the six sections of the deck as a numbered chapter list.
content_blocks.content_instruction: Six entries, each with a number badge, a Title Case section name, and a one-line description in medium blue-grey: 01 'Problem Definition' — 'A fragmented path into society.' / 02 'Solution and Differentiation' — 'YouthPath and what makes it different.' / 03 'System Design' — 'A four-layer architecture with a multi-agent core.' / 04 'Evaluation Plan' — 'Quantitative and qualitative dual-track evaluation.' / 05 'Execution Plan' — 'Team, schedule, and cost.' / 06 'Expected Outcomes' — 'User impact and a five-phase roadmap.'
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Stacked vertically inside the right 2/3 of the slide. All six number badges share the same x-coordinate. Even vertical spacing between rows.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Number badges as deep-navy filled squares with bold white numerals, identical to the deck's standard. Section name in Pretendard Bold deep navy. Description in Pretendard Regular medium blue-grey, smaller.

content_blocks.content_instruction: '02' followed by a small deep-navy accent square.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Bottom-right corner.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Small Pretendard Bold deep navy. Accent square ~4–6px filled deep navy.

layout_instructions.structure: Two-column asymmetric: left 33% lavender panel holding the chapter-header title, right 67% holding the six-row numbered list.

header_body_footer.header: Thin deep-navy bar across the very top. The 'CONTENTS' kicker sits in the top-right. The left panel's pale lavender extends up to meet the header bar so the panel and header read as connected.
header_body_footer.body: Left 33% panel: large 'Contents' title and small ALL CAPS subtitle stacked. Right 67%: six numbered rows, each with badge + title + description.
header_body_footer.footer: Page number '02' bottom-right with a small navy accent square. Bottom-left empty.

reading_order: Numbered list rows 01 through 06 (top to bottom)
layout_instructions.alignment: Left panel content is left-aligned to a left margin inside the panel. Numbered rows are left-aligned at the badge x-coordinate.
layout_instructions.spacing: Even vertical rhythm between the six rows. Generous whitespace within the left panel — the panel should feel airy, not cramped.
layout_instructions.density: Low to medium. This is a navigation page, not a content page.
layout_instructions.body_slide_consistency: Even though this slide uses the left-panel variant, it still uses the same right-aligned ALL CAPS kicker, the same number-badge style, the same page-number block, and the same color palette as every other body slide.

palette_rules: Left panel fill is pale lavender (~#C9D1E5 approx).
Number badges are deep navy with white numerals — no other color.
Description text is medium blue-grey, never deep navy.
typography_rules: The 'Contents' word is the largest type element on the slide.
ALL CAPS kicker text always uses loosened letter-spacing.
Section names in title case, never ALL CAPS.
component_rules: All six number badges are identical in size, fill color, and font weight.
All six rows use identical row height and identical type sizes.
icon_rules: No icons. Number badges are the only visual cueing.
infographic_rules: The numbered list is the only infographic element.
diagram_rules: No diagrams.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Adding decorative dots, bullets, or hairlines beside each row.
Adding a thumbnail preview of each section's slide.
Using more than six items.
Adding the diagonal overlay (this is a body slide).
Using ALL CAPS for the section names.

evidence_to_use: Section structure across the slide_plan.json (slides 3–12 grouped into six topic_groups)
evidence_to_avoid: Detailed proposal content — the contents page summarizes structure, not arguments.

generator_notes.ppt_generation_note: Build the left panel as a single rectangle filled with the lavender swatch, extending from below the navy header bar to the bottom of the slide. The right column is a simple list, not a multi-column layout.
generator_notes.image_generation_note: Render the number badges as filled squares with sharp 90-degree corners, not rounded.
generator_notes.fallback_if_content_is_sparse: If the generator wants to add icons next to each row, suppress them — DESIGN.md restricts icons to card-corner placement only.

slides.slide_title: Problem Definition — A Fragmented Path into Society

prompt.objective: Establish the problem at scale (~12 million Korean youth) and shape (four fragmented information domains) in a single visually anchored frame.
prompt.narrative_function: First substantive slide of the argument. It earns the macro half of the 20-point Problem Definition rubric. Pairs with slide 4 (the micro view) as a two-part problem statement.
prompt.visual_intent: Asymmetric two-zone composition: a large emphasized number on the left grounds the slide's scale claim, and a 2x2 card grid on the right itemizes the four fragmented domains. The viewer should grasp 'big number + four pieces' in under five seconds.

content_blocks.content_instruction: 'Problem Definition'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Top-left of the slide, below the navy header bar.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Pretendard ExtraBold, large, deep navy, title case, left-aligned.

content_blocks.purpose: Right-aligned ALL CAPS section kicker (standard body-slide element).
content_blocks.content_instruction: 'PROBLEM DEFINITION'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Top-right corner.
content_blocks.style_instruction: ALL CAPS, small Bold deep navy, right-aligned.

content_blocks.purpose: Two-line right-aligned descriptive subtitle (standard body-slide element).
content_blocks.content_instruction: Line 1: 'Youth career-entry information is fragmented,' Line 2: 'and qualification matching, real-time data, and alerts are all missing.'
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Directly below the kicker, right-aligned.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Small Pretendard Regular, medium blue-grey, two lines exactly, right-aligned.

content_blocks.purpose: Anchor the left side with a single emphasized scale figure.
content_blocks.content_instruction: Large numeral '~12M' as the headline. Below it: small label 'Korean youth aged 19–34'. Below that: a single short sentence 'Average information-search cost: 2 hours.'
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Left ~40% of the body zone, vertically centered.
content_blocks.style_instruction: '~12M' in Pretendard ExtraBold deep navy, sized at roughly 25% of the slide width. Label in medium blue-grey ALL CAPS small Bold. Sentence in deep navy Regular.

content_blocks.purpose: Itemize the four fragmented domains as parallel cards.
content_blocks.content_instruction: A 2x2 grid of four cards. Each card contains: domain name (bold heading) / existing platform names (one line, smaller) / one-line limitation. Card 1: 'Youth Policy' / 'Government24, Ontongchungnyeon' / 'List-only search; no eligibility match.' Card 2: 'Job Postings' / 'WorkNet, JobKorea, Saramin' / 'Per-employer fragmentation; no resume linkage.' Card 3: 'Resume and Interview' / 'Standalone tools' / 'No company-specific reference data.' Card 4: 'Schedule Management' / 'Personal calendar apps' / 'No automatic deadline sync.'
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Right ~60% of the body zone. 2x2 grid, even spacing.
content_blocks.style_instruction: White-fill cards with thin lavender-grey outline. Domain name in Pretendard Bold deep navy. Platform list in medium blue-grey small Regular. Limitation in deep navy Regular. One small line icon per card in the top-right corner.

content_blocks.content_instruction: Card 1 = document icon. Card 2 = briefcase icon. Card 3 = pen icon. Card 4 = calendar icon.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Top-right corner of each card, small.
content_blocks.style_instruction: All four icons share the same line weight and the same deep-navy color. Outline-only style, never filled.

content_blocks.content_instruction: '03' with a small deep-navy accent square.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Bottom-right corner.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Small Pretendard Bold deep navy. Accent square ~4–6px filled deep navy.

layout_instructions.structure: Asymmetric two-column body: left ~40% (single emphasized number block), right ~60% (2x2 card grid).

header_body_footer.header: Standard body-slide header: navy bar, 'Problem Definition' title left, 'PROBLEM DEFINITION' kicker top-right, two-line subtitle below the kicker.
header_body_footer.body: Left: '~12M' emphasized number with supporting label and sentence. Right: 2x2 grid of four white cards, one per domain.
header_body_footer.footer: Page number '03' bottom-right with navy accent square. Bottom-left empty.

layout_instructions.alignment: All four cards align to the same baseline grid; identical card width and height. The big number block aligns to the left edge of the body zone.
layout_instructions.spacing: Generous whitespace between the title and the body. Inter-card gap roughly 8–10% of card width. Vertical breathing room between the big number and the supporting label.
layout_instructions.body_slide_consistency: Header zone is identical in geometry to slides 4–12. Footer is identical to slides 2 and 4–12. Card outline weight, corner radius, and internal padding established here will be reused on slides 4, 6, 9, 10, 12.

palette_rules: Big number is deep navy on the canvas — no fill behind it.
Cards are white with thin lavender-grey outlines.
No fill colors inside the cards beyond white.
typography_rules: The big number '~12M' is the largest type element on this slide but not as large as the cover title.
Card headings use title case in Bold, not ALL CAPS.
Card platform-name lists are smaller than card headings and in medium blue-grey.
component_rules: All four cards are visually identical in size, outline weight, padding, and icon size.
Number badges are not used on this slide — the cards are domain cards, not numbered cards.
table_rules: No tables — cards are intentionally chosen instead.
icon_rules: Exactly one icon per card, top-right corner.
Outline icons only, single line weight, single color.
Icons must read as semantic cues for the domain, not as decoration.
infographic_rules: The 2x2 card grid is the slide's primary infographic — keep it visually clean.
No connecting lines between the cards or between the cards and the big number.
diagram_rules: No diagrams.
imagery_rules: No photographs or illustrations beyond the four card icons.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Putting the '~12M' number inside a card or behind a colored fill.
Adding a fifth card to the grid.
Using bar-chart-style visualizations of the platform list.
Decorating the cards with corner ribbons or starbursts.
Using filled icons or multicolor icons.

must_include: All four domains and their existing-platform examples
must_not_include: Per-platform user counts not stated in the proposal
Welfare-coverage gap statistics not stated in the proposal
Any pain-point details (those belong on slide 4)
evidence_to_avoid: F1's pain-point list — that is slide 4's territory.
F4's competitive comparison — that is slide 6's territory.

generator_notes.ppt_generation_note: Render '~12M' as a single text element, not as an image — keep it editable so the team can adjust the figure if a more recent statistic is found.
generator_notes.image_generation_note: Render the four cards with hairline outlines — about 0.5–0.75px equivalent — to keep the report-like restraint.
generator_notes.fallback_if_content_is_sparse: If platform names per card are unknown, default to leaving the platform line empty rather than inventing names.

slides.slide_title: Pain Points — Four Frictions a Single Tool Cannot Fix

prompt.objective: Show the four lived pain points that compound on each other and explain why no single existing tool resolves them.
prompt.narrative_function: Continuation of slide 3 (split topic). Slide 3 covered the macro view (scale and domains); this slide covers the micro view (the user's lived friction). Together they earn the 20-point Problem Definition rubric.
prompt.visual_intent: A clean 2x2 numbered card grid. The viewer should perceive four parallel, equally weighted problems that together form a structural barrier. No single card should visually dominate.

content_blocks.content_instruction: 'Pain Points'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Top-left, below the navy header bar.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Pretendard ExtraBold large deep navy title case left-aligned.

content_blocks.content_instruction: 'PAIN POINTS'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Top-right corner.
content_blocks.style_instruction: ALL CAPS small Bold deep navy right-aligned.

content_blocks.content_instruction: Line 1: 'Four structural problems compound on each other,' Line 2: 'so no single existing tool can resolve them.'
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Below kicker, right-aligned, two lines exactly.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Small Regular medium blue-grey right-aligned.

content_blocks.purpose: Itemize the four pain points as numbered, equally weighted cards.
content_blocks.content_instruction: A 2x2 grid of four cards. Each card contains: a deep-navy number badge tucked into the top-left corner (01–04), a bold heading, a one-line description, and a small emphasized fact. Card 01 'Information Fragmentation' / 'Information lives across 5–6 separate platforms.' / fact: '~2 hours per task'. Card 02 'No Qualification Matching' / 'Policy sites list policies but never decide whether you qualify.' / fact: 'Manual eligibility check'. Card 03 'LLM Knowledge Cutoff' / 'General-purpose LLMs cannot see new policies, filings, or postings.' / fact: 'Stale answers'. Card 04 'Missed Application Deadlines' / 'Deadlines are scattered; users miss eligible policies.' / fact: 'No unified alerts'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Body zone, 2x2 grid, even spacing. All four cards identical in size.
content_blocks.style_instruction: White-fill cards with thin lavender-grey outline. Number badge tucked outside the top-left card corner, slightly overlapping it. Heading in Pretendard Bold deep navy. Description in Pretendard Regular deep navy. Fact in medium blue-grey ALL CAPS small Bold.

content_blocks.content_instruction: '04' with a small deep-navy accent square.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Bottom-right.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Small Pretendard Bold deep navy.

layout_instructions.structure: Body zone is a single 2x2 grid. No left/right division.

header_body_footer.header: Standard: navy bar, 'Pain Points' title left, 'PAIN POINTS' kicker top-right, two-line subtitle below.
header_body_footer.body: 2x2 grid of four numbered cards filling the body zone.
header_body_footer.footer: Page number '04' bottom-right with accent square.

reading_order: Cards in z-order: 01 top-left, 02 top-right, 03 bottom-left, 04 bottom-right
layout_instructions.alignment: All four cards share identical width, height, padding, and badge placement. The grid is perfectly symmetric.
layout_instructions.spacing: Inter-card gap ~8–10% of card width, both horizontally and vertically.
layout_instructions.body_slide_consistency: Card outline, corner radius, internal padding, badge style match slide 3 exactly. Number badges here use the same fill, size, and numeral weight as the badges on slide 2 (contents) and slide 6 (differentiators). Header and footer are identical to slides 3 and 5–12.

palette_rules: Cards: white fill, lavender-grey outline.
Number badge: deep navy fill with white numeral.
Fact text: medium blue-grey only.
No filled accent on the cards beyond the number badges.
typography_rules: Card headings use title case Bold, not ALL CAPS.
Fact lines use ALL CAPS small Bold for visual punctuation.
Description lines use Regular weight, deep navy.
component_rules: All four number badges are identical in size, corner radius, and fill.
Badges sit slightly outside the card's top-left corner, overlapping by ~25–30% of the badge size.
icon_rules: Icons are optional on this slide.
If used, exactly one outline icon per card in the top-right corner, single line weight, single deep-navy color, identical size across cards.
If not used, the card layout is sufficient on its own.
infographic_rules: The 2x2 numbered card grid is the slide's only infographic system.
No connecting lines between cards.
No background tint behind the grid.
diagram_rules: No diagrams.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Making one card visually larger than the others.
Adding a fifth or sixth pain point.
Using a colored badge for the most-important pain point — all four are equally weighted.
Using filled icons or multicolor icons.
Adding a 'CTA' or 'next step' card to the grid.

must_include: A short emphasized fact line per card
must_not_include: User-interview quotes (none exist in the source proposal)
Pain points beyond the four in the source
evidence_to_use: F1-context-pain-points (four pain points map directly to the four cards)

generator_notes.ppt_generation_note: If the generator supports it, group each card as a single object so the four cards can be re-flowed as a unit if the slide aspect ratio changes.
generator_notes.image_generation_note: Keep card outlines hairline. Avoid drop shadows.
generator_notes.fallback_if_content_is_sparse: If a 'fact' line cannot be sourced for a given card, omit it rather than inventing one.

slides.slide_title: Solution — One Profile, Four Domains, One Interface

prompt.objective: Introduce YouthPath's value proposition and reveal that the solution mirrors the problem's shape — four agents, one per fragmented domain — anchored by a single central hub.
prompt.narrative_function: The pivot point of the deck. The audience moves from problem framing to solution framing. The visual mirror of slide 3's four domains is intentional and should feel inevitable.
prompt.visual_intent: A central deep-navy circle as the visual anchor with four agent cards in symmetric quadrants around it. The eye should move outward from center to cards in radial fashion. The diagram should not feel mechanical or templated — proportions matter.

content_blocks.content_instruction: 'Solution'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Top-left.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Pretendard ExtraBold large deep navy title case left-aligned.

content_blocks.content_instruction: 'SOLUTION OVERVIEW'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Top-right corner.
content_blocks.style_instruction: ALL CAPS small Bold deep navy right-aligned.

content_blocks.content_instruction: Line 1: 'One profile entry, four domains.' Line 2: 'A single interface replaces five to six platform visits.'

slides.slide_title: Execution Plan — Team, Schedule, and Cost

prompt.objective: Compress feasibility (team + schedule + cost + risk) into a single visually anchored slide whose lead is a seven-week Gantt chart.
prompt.narrative_function: Earns the 15-point Feasibility rubric. Single slide because a 7–10 minute video cannot afford four separate feasibility pages.
prompt.visual_intent: The Gantt chart dominates the body zone (~60% of the height). Cost and risk are deliberately compressed into a quiet supporting block at the bottom (~40%). The visual hierarchy must read 'Gantt is the headline; cost and risk are the footnote.'

content_blocks.placement_instruction: Top-right.
content_blocks.style_instruction: ALL CAPS small Bold deep navy right-aligned.

content_blocks.placement_instruction: Below kicker, right-aligned, two lines exactly.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Small Regular medium blue-grey right-aligned.

content_blocks.purpose: Seven-week Gantt chart with five workstream rows.
content_blocks.content_instruction: Header row (deep navy fill with white text): 'Workstream' | 'W10' | 'W11' | 'W12' | 'W13' | 'W14' | 'W15' | 'W16'. Five body rows (one per team member's workstream): Row A 'Policy + Static RAG' (active W11–W14, peak W12); Row B 'Job + DART/MCP' (active W11–W14, peak W13); Row C 'Resume + Evaluation' (active W11–W15, peak W15); Row D 'Frontend + UX' (active W12–W15, peak W14); Row E 'Backend + LangGraph' (active W11–W15, peak W13–W14). Above the workstream rows or below them, also mark the major milestones: W10 'Proposal Talk', W11 'Data + RAG', W12 'Single-Agent Prototype', W13 '4-Agent Integration', W14 'FE/BE Integration', W15 'Evaluation Experiments', W16 'Final Demo'.
content_blocks.placement_instruction: Top ~60% of the body zone.
content_blocks.style_instruction: Header row: deep navy fill with white Bold ALL CAPS centered. Workstream labels left-aligned in deep navy Bold small. Bars: filled rectangles in varying lavender intensities (peak weeks at deepest navy, off-peak weeks at pale lavender, inactive weeks empty). Bar text inside bars in white or deep navy depending on the bar's intensity. Grid lines hairline lavender-grey, horizontal only.

content_blocks.content_instruction: Headline: '~$52 total'. Breakdown: 'LUXIA $50 (course-provided) + GPT-4o-mini $2'. One supporting line: 'AWS Academy / open-source tooling at $0.'

content_blocks.style_instruction: Plain text composition, no boxed callout. Headline in Pretendard ExtraBold large deep navy. Breakdown and supporting line in Regular small deep navy and medium blue-grey.

layout_instructions.spacing: Tight row height in the Gantt to fit five workstreams comfortably. Modest gap between the Gantt and the bottom block.
layout_instructions.body_slide_consistency: Gantt header style is identical to slide 6 and slide 10's table headers — the same deep-navy fill with white centered ALL CAPS. palette_rules: Gantt bars: varying intensities of lavender and navy only — no other accent.

Risk items in Regular small.
component_rules: All week columns are equal width.
All bar heights identical.
Risk card padding matches other cards in the deck.
chart_rules: The Gantt is the only chart-like element. No additional charts.
Bar intensity should map to workload level — deeper navy = peak week, paler lavender = light involvement, blank = no involvement.
Do not add a percentage-complete shading inside bars.
table_rules: Gantt body grid is horizontal-only hairlines.
Header row uses centered ALL CAPS Bold white text on deep navy fill.
icon_rules: No icons on the Gantt chart.
No icons in the cost block.
No icon on the risk card (header text alone is sufficient).
infographic_rules: Gantt is the headline; cost and risk are subordinate.
Do not promote either bottom block visually above the Gantt.
diagram_rules: No diagrams on this slide.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Adding milestone diamond markers in a third color (e.g., red) to the Gantt.
Drawing a budget pie chart for a $52 budget (would be silly).
Listing risk mitigations on the slide (those belong in the spoken script).
Adding a sixth workstream that doesn't exist in the team plan.
Using percentage-complete fills inside bars (the project hasn't started).

must_not_include: The full Appendix C team-learning plan

generator_notes.fallback_if_content_is_sparse: If a workstream's exact week range is uncertain, default to a conservative range that aligns with the major milestones.

prompt.visual_intent: Quiet closing page. The viewer's last impression should be a tidy thank-you message and the team's affiliation. Background diagonal triangles return from slide 1 to close the visual loop.

layout_instructions.structure: Single composition with the closing message centered, a small subtitle beneath it, a three-column metadata strip at the bottom under a thin divider, and a diagonal overlay echo in the background.

header_body_footer.body: Closing message centered with a small ALL CAPS subtitle directly beneath. Diagonal triangles in the background.
header_body_footer.footer: Full-width thin lavender-grey divider, then a three-column metadata strip.

layout_instructions.alignment: Closing message and subtitle share the same horizontal alignment. Metadata columns evenly spaced.
layout_instructions.spacing: Generous whitespace around the closing message.
layout_instructions.body_slide_consistency: The closing slide explicitly breaks out of the body-slide system (no two-line subtitle in the header zone, no left-aligned title). This is intentional and matches the cover's break from the body system. Page number is omitted (matching the cover) or rendered very small at the designer's discretion.

palette_rules: Diagonal triangles in pale lavender — same swatch as slide 1.
Closing message in deep navy.
No new colors introduced on the closing page.
typography_rules: 'Thank You' line is the largest type element.
ALL CAPS subtitle uses loosened tracking.
Metadata strip in Regular small.
component_rules: Lavender-grey divider is hairline weight.

infographic_rules: No infographic content.
diagram_rules: No diagrams.
imagery_rules: No team photo, no logo, no QR code.

Adding a QR code or social-media icon strip.
Adding a team photo.
Repeating the cover's title.

Showing a slide-deck miniaturization preview.
